cot_prefix: Okay, I need to determine which of the two statements is sarcastic. Let me analyze both options carefully. Option (A): "One of the worst things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." This statement is straightforward. When someone says something is "one of the worst things," they are expressing genuine dislike for the activity. There's no obvious sarcasm here, just a direct statement of what the person found unpleasant. Option (B): "One of the enjoyable things to do working in retail was pushing a trolley of returns around the store putting them back." This statement is more interesting. The speaker is saying that pushing a trolley of returns was an "enjoyable" activity. However, in reality, pushing a trolley of returns in retail is generally considered a boring or tedious task. Most people would not find it enjoyable, so when someone says they did enjoy it, it's likely sarcastic or ironic.
